title: Repository baseline
decision: Treat GitHub as pre-Sprint-0. No product feature work before S0-001…S0-018 passes quality gates.

title: Application architecture
decision: Modular monolith in one Next.js + TypeScript application. Domain modules own business logic and persistence access; cross-domain calls go through public module services/events.

title: Persistence stack
decision: PostgreSQL + Prisma. Supabase Postgres is the initial hosting target. Database remains portable PostgreSQL; vendor-specific features require an adapter/ADR.

title: Authentication
decision: Supabase Auth is identity provider. Application User is canonical business identity. Email and verified phone identities may link to one User; no custom password store.

title: Authorization
decision: Hybrid RBAC + capability grants. Coarse RoleKey is stable; fine-grained operational access uses Capability and ScopedGrant. Every server mutation checks capability and object scope.

title: Route namespaces
decision: Public pages live outside protected shells; authenticated product under /app; privileged operations under /admin. Role workspaces are subtrees of /app.

title: Visual source precedence
decision: The current Site.zip corpus is visual baseline. Dedicated canonical sets supersede older domain generations; older sets remain reference for missing states/capabilities.

title: Brand tokens
decision: Set50 is canonical for production visual tokens. Sprint 0 token values are deprecated starter values. Canonical colors: Copper #C6683D; Deep Green #163A2F; Ivory #FAF7F2; Sage #A8BFAE; Sand #F1E9D8; Stone #D9D5CC; Charcoal #1F1F1F; Success #2E7D4F; Warning #E2A23B; Error #D64545; Info #4A7BD3.

title: Logo system
decision: Set50/510 is canonical. Approved Primary Logo may use Huddle wordmark + icon lockup exactly as supplied. Do not recreate or alter geometry. Sprint 0 prohibition on lockup is superseded.

title: Typography implementation
decision: Visual metrics follow set50. Use a production-safe system UI stack by default: ui-sans-serif, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif. Do not bundle or redistribute SF Pro font files. H1 32/700, H2 24/600, H3 20/600, body large 16/400, body regular 14/400, body small 12/400.

title: Spacing and responsive system
decision: Set50 8pt scale: 4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 64px. Semantic viewports: compact 0–767, medium 768–1199, wide >=1200. Set61 is responsive behavior reference; mobile sets target responsive web first.

title: Mobile strategy
decision: P0/P1 is responsive web/PWA-capable web. Native mobile is a separate future program. Sets34/43/52/57 are interaction and compact-layout references, not authorization to create native clients in current roadmap.

title: Marketplace boundaries
decision: Separate Listings, Catalog & Partner Feed, Orders & Fulfillment, Payments & Settlement, and Affiliate Tracking. Never create a single MarketplaceItem aggregate/table.

title: Experience model
decision: Use shared Experience aggregate for discoverable scheduled offerings with ExperienceKind EVENT|ACTIVITY, shared occurrence/registration primitives, and optional EventDetails/ActivityDetails extensions. Community events are Experience with community context.

title: Messaging core
decision: One Conversation/Participant/Message core supports direct, group, listing, booking, event and support contexts. Context policies determine who may create, join, message, attach media, or report.

title: Realtime
decision: Use an application RealtimeGateway abstraction. Initial adapter may use Supabase Realtime; domain services remain provider-neutral. Persist messages/notifications before broadcast; reconnect and deduplicate by event id.

title: Notification delivery
decision: Separate Notification (user-facing intent), NotificationDelivery (per-channel attempt), NotificationPreference, and Template. Channels: in-app, email, push. Adapters are idempotent and retryable.

title: Search
decision: P0 uses PostgreSQL full-text/trigram search behind SearchProvider interface. External search engine may replace/augment later without changing domain APIs. Sensitive/private entities are filtered before result serialization.

title: Payments boundary
decision: Build provider-neutral PaymentProvider and SettlementProvider interfaces, immutable internal transaction references, idempotency keys and webhook inbox. No live charges/payouts until merchant-of-record, marketplace liability, KYC and provider ADR are approved.

title: Family matching privacy
decision: Privacy by default: no child exact birthdate in discovery, no exact home address, coarse location only, explicit parent consent for matching, reciprocal connection before unrestricted chat, block/report controls on every connection surface.

title: Municipality analytics privacy
decision: Only aggregate/de-identified outputs. Default minimum cohort threshold 10, suppress smaller cells, no row-level export, no direct identifiers, scoped municipality geography, auditable export approvals. Threshold is configuration but cannot be disabled in production.

title: Localization and SEO
decision: Public SEO pages use explicit locale prefixes /en and /no with canonical/hreflang. Authenticated /app routes are locale-neutral and use user preference. No translated slug without canonical mapping.

title: Trust and safety generation
decision: Set56 is canonical moderation operations UI; set22 is reference for unique capabilities. Trust & Safety owns reports/cases/enforcement/appeals.

title: Operational admin scope
decision: Sets26–29 are P2/P3. Do not ship controls that are visual-only; each admin control requires a functioning backend capability, audit event, permission and rollback path.

title: Legal content authority
decision: Set30 is UX/template reference only. Production legal copy must come from separately approved legal source and be versioned.

title: Artifact traceability
decision: Keep source artifact IDs stable. Archive exact duplicate set54. Reserve absent numbering 221–230. Never renumber source screens.

title: Audit and domain events
decision: Every privileged or security-sensitive mutation writes AuditLog. Cross-domain side effects use transactional outbox/domain-event pattern; consumers are idempotent.

title: Media storage
decision: Supabase Storage initial target behind MediaStorage interface. Uploads use signed URLs, MIME/size allowlists, malware scanning hook, image metadata stripping, ownership checks and quarantine state.

title: Analytics event contract
decision: Use versioned product-event taxonomy. Never send child identifiers, message text, exact address or sensitive free text to analytics. Server events are authoritative for transactions/security.

title: Soft delete and retention
decision: Soft delete only where recovery/audit is required. Financial/audit records are append-only; privacy deletion uses tombstoning/anonymization according to retention policy and legal hold.
